data.title: Quarterly Options Expiry Shows Mixed Results for BTC and ETH
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, Greeks.live analyst Adam shared insights on social media regarding the options expiry data for March 28. A total of 139,000 BTC options expired with a Put Call Ratio of 0.49, a maximum pain point at $85,000, and a nominal value of $12.1 billion. Additionally, 301,000 ETH options expired with a Put Call Ratio of 0.39, a maximum pain point at $2,400, and a nominal value of $2.13 billion.

This week marks the quarterly expiry, and the first quarter's market performance fell short of most expectations, with significant volatility leading to a close at the lower end of the price range. The expiry volume accounted for over 40% of total open interest, with BTC options making up nearly 80% of the total expiry volume, while ETH options comprised nearly 20%. Among other cryptocurrencies, Solana had the largest share at only 2%. Despite skepticism surrounding ETH, the market structure of holdings remains stable.

Implied volatility (IV) saw a slight decrease, with BTC's main term volatility dropping below 50% and ETH's main term volatility hovering around 60%. The cryptocurrency market currently lacks new capital inflows and narratives, leading to a dull investor sentiment. Post-quarterly expiry, there are no immediate signs of a reversal, and large investors are increasingly engaging in dual selling strategies. Options market-making institutions are intensifying their selling efforts, indicating further short-term IV decline, which may pose challenges for buyers moving forward.

data.title: New Wallet Sells 3,433 ETH for 6.81 Million DAI
data.description: According to BlockBeats, Lookonchain monitoring has revealed that a newly created wallet received 3,433 ETH from THORChain. The wallet subsequently sold the ETH at an average price of $1,981, resulting in a total of 6.81 million DAI.

data.title: China's Foreign Exchange Bureau to Enhance Monitoring of Cryptocurrency Impact
data.description: According to Foresight News, Li Bin, Deputy Director of China's State Administration of Foreign Exchange, emphasized the importance of monitoring the impact of cryptocurrencies on capital flows. Speaking at the Boao Forum for Asia's sub-forum on 'Seeking Monetary and Financial Stability in an Unstable World,' Li highlighted the bureau's ongoing efforts to improve its capabilities in identifying and monitoring these effects. He noted that this is a crucial area of study and that further research will be conducted to enhance related initiatives.

data.title: Terra creditor claims portal scheduled to open on March 31
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Terra announced that its creditor claims portal will be open on March 31, and the submission deadline is April 30. Users must first complete the registration, provide proof of ownership (such as wallet address and read-only API key) or other required supporting documents, and then submit the claim form.

data.title: A trader deposited 6.14 million MUBARAK into Binance 7 hours ago, earning about $760,000
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to Lookonchain monitoring, a trader spent US$2,861 to purchase 6.14 million MUBARAK on March 13, and deposited it in Binance 7 hours ago, making a profit of about US$760,000.

data.title: Bitget Wallet releases "On-chain Report" to provide insights into the potential of crypto payments and industry challenges
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Web3 wallet Bitget Wallet released the latest "On-chain Report", revealing the development potential and popularization challenges of crypto payments, providing important insights for the industry. The report shows that 46% of the surveyed users prefer crypto payments due to transaction speed, while 37% believe that security risks are the main concern. This survey covers 4,599 users worldwide, analyzes the development trends of crypto payments in different regions and generations, and emphasizes the advantages of crypto payments in accelerating financial inclusion and cross-border transactions.
Bitget Wallet proposed the PayFi vision, integrating income, transfers and consumption to create a seamless on-chain financial ecosystem. By combining DeFi income with real-world payment scenarios, Bitget Wallet is promoting crypto payments from speculative assets to everyday financial tools, providing global users with a one-stop digital financial experience.

data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Drops Below 86,000 USDT with a 1.75% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Mar 28, 2025, 04:56 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has dropped below 86,000 USDT and is now trading at 85,966 USDT, with a narrowed 1.75% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: Terraform Labs Opens Portal for Crypto Asset Loss Claims
data.description: According to PANews, Terraform Labs has announced the opening of its crypto asset loss claims portal on March 31, 2025. Creditors can submit eligible claims through the portal at http://claims.terra.money. Required documentation includes wallet addresses or read-only API keys, along with a completed claim form. The deadline for submission is April 30, 2025, at 23:59 Eastern Time. Late submissions will not be accepted. Creditors are advised to carefully review the claim procedures detailed on the portal. For assistance, they can contact Kroll Restructuring Administration at Terraforminfo@ra.kroll.com.

data.title: BTC falls below 86,000 USDT
data.description: OKX market data shows that BTC fell below 86,000 USDT and is now at 85,966.5 USDT, a 24-hour drop of 1.74%. (This newsletter is generated with the assistance of AI)
